Bank Churn Prediction

Problem Statement¶

Context¶

Businesses like banks which provide service have to worry about problem of 'Customer Churn' i.e. customers leaving and joining another service provider. It is important to understand which aspects of the service influence a customer's decision in this regard. Management can concentrate efforts on improvement of service, keeping in mind these priorities.

Objective¶

You as a Data scientist with the bank need to build a neural network based classifier that can determine whether a customer will leave the bank or not in the next 6 months.

Data Dictionary¶

  • CustomerId: Unique ID which is assigned to each customer

  • Surname: Last name of the customer

  • CreditScore: It defines the credit history of the customer.

  • Geography: A customer’s location

  • Gender: It defines the Gender of the customer

  • Age: Age of the customer

  • Tenure: Number of years for which the customer has been with the bank

  • NumOfProducts: refers to the number of products that a customer has purchased through the bank.

  • Balance: Account balance

  • HasCrCard: It is a categorical variable which decides whether the customer has credit card or not.

  • EstimatedSalary: Estimated salary

  • isActiveMember: Is is a categorical variable which decides whether the customer is active member of the bank or not ( Active member in the sense, using bank products regularly, making transactions etc )

  • Exited : whether or not the customer left the bank within six month. It can take two values ** 0=No ( Customer did not leave the bank ) ** 1=Yes ( Customer left the bank )

Disclaimer NOTE: Code blocks seen below are taken from course related primary learning and additional learnng notebooks

Definition of Problem Statement:

  • The problem faced by the bank is three fold:
  • Customer Churn of the bank is at a high enough level that is damaging to the banks top ine revenue generation
  • The expenditure taken to prevent churn can become prohibitively leading to loss when taken in the aggregate over time and the percentage of the customer base targeted with rentention tactics
  • The efforts needed to prevent churn and/or the potential backlog that prevents the allocation of resources to prevent customer attrition in a timely matter, requires an assessment to be made 6 months in advance, when the transparency of churn and the likelihood of those tactics to succeed can be highly unclear at best still. However, efforts have to be taken at that time as rentention efforts initiated on a short time horizion, say 3 months, may result in lower rentention and higher losses incurred as the offsetting revenue for rentition expenditure is not captured/obtained.

In [ ]:
# Libraries to help with reading and manipulating data

import pandas as pd  # Library for data manipulation and analysis.
import numpy as np   # Fundamental package for scientific computing.
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt  # Plotting library for creating visualizations.
import seaborn as sns #For advanced visualizations.

from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split  # Function for splitting datasets for training and testing.
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler # For nomralizing the data set to improve ability to use the data within the NN
from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score, recall_score, precision_score, f1_score

import time  # Module for time-related operations.

import tensorflow as tf #An end-to-end open source machine learning platform
from tensorflow import keras  # High-level neural networks API for deep learning.
from keras_tuner.tuners import RandomSearch  # Hyperparameter tuning for neural networks. # Changed kerastuner to keras_tuner
import keras_tuner as kt # Used to perform Iterative Tuning of the hyperparameters fo the NN's
from keras import backend   # Abstraction layer for neural network backend engines.
from keras.models import Sequential  # Model for building NN sequentially.
from keras.layers import Dense,Dropout,BatchNormalization   # for creating fully connected neural network layers.

import random #Used for number randomization

# importing SMOTE
from imblearn.over_sampling import SMOTE #Used for purposes of balancing the dataset via oversampling

# importing metrics
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix,roc_curve,classification_report,recall_score

import time # To record the start, stop, and differential time taken for the NN to be fit to the training and validation data sets

# Library to avoid the warnings
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore")

Observations:

  • Row Number and CustomerID provide no value and should be removed from the data set via processing

  • Estimated Salary and Balance have a high number of unique values, but those numbers are quite reflective of actual real world conditions

  • Surname will also have to be dropped as the information gain and resulting insights is bot be worth the conputational resource allocation, especially since those values will have to be encoded leading to roughly 2931 extra feature columns to be added to the dataset!

Exploratory Data Analysis¶

General Note:

  • The 3 features previously mentioned in comments/observations above will not have any uni or bivariate analysis conducted on them below
In [280]:
# function to plot a boxplot and a histogram along the same scale.


def histogram_boxplot(data, feature, figsize=(12, 7), kde=False, bins=None):
    """
    Boxplot and histogram combined

    data: dataframe
    feature: dataframe column
    figsize: size of figure (default (12,7))
    kde: whether to show the density curve (default False)
    bins: number of bins for histogram (default None)
    """
    f2, (ax_box2, ax_hist2) = plt.subplots(
        nrows=2,  # Number of rows of the subplot grid= 2
        sharex=True,  # x-axis will be shared among all subplots
        gridspec_kw={"height_ratios": (0.25, 0.75)},
        figsize=figsize,
    )  # creating the 2 subplots
    sns.boxplot(
        data=data, x=feature, ax=ax_box2, showmeans=True, color="violet"
    )  # boxplot will be created and a star will indicate the mean value of the column
    sns.histplot(
        data=data, x=feature, kde=kde, ax=ax_hist2, bins=bins, palette="winter"
    ) if bins else sns.histplot(
        data=data, x=feature, kde=kde, ax=ax_hist2
    )  # For histogram
    ax_hist2.axvline(
        data[feature].mean(), color="green", linestyle="--"
    )  # Add mean to the histogram
    ax_hist2.axvline(
        data[feature].median(), color="black", linestyle="-"
    )  # Add median to the histogram
In [281]:
# function to create labeled barplots


def labeled_barplot(data, feature, perc=False, n=None):
    """
    Barplot with percentage at the top

    data: dataframe
    feature: dataframe column
    perc: whether to display percentages instead of count (default is False)
    n: displays the top n category levels (default is None, i.e., display all levels)
    """

    total = len(data[feature])  # length of the column
    count = data[feature].nunique()
    if n is None:
        plt.figure(figsize=(count + 1, 5))
    else:
        plt.figure(figsize=(n + 1, 5))

    plt.xticks(rotation=90, fontsize=15)
    ax = sns.countplot(
        data=data,
        x=feature,
        palette="Paired",
        order=data[feature].value_counts().index[:n].sort_values(),
    )

    for p in ax.patches:
        if perc == True:
            label = "{:.1f}%".format(
                100 * p.get_height() / total
            )  # percentage of each class of the category
        else:
            label = p.get_height()  # count of each level of the category

        x = p.get_x() + p.get_width() / 2  # width of the plot
        y = p.get_height()  # height of the plot

        ax.annotate(
            label,
            (x, y),
            ha="center",
            va="center",
            size=12,
            xytext=(0, 5),
            textcoords="offset points",
        )  # annotate the percentage

    plt.show()  # show the plot
In [282]:
# function to plot stacked bar chart


def stacked_barplot(data, predictor, target):
    """
    Print the category counts and plot a stacked bar chart

    data: dataframe
    predictor: independent variable
    target: target variable
    """
    count = data[predictor].nunique()
    sorter = data[target].value_counts().index[-1]
    tab1 = pd.crosstab(data[predictor], data[target], margins=True).sort_values(
        by=sorter, ascending=False
    )
    print(tab1)
    print("-" * 120)
    tab = pd.crosstab(data[predictor], data[target], normalize="index").sort_values(
        by=sorter, ascending=False
    )
    tab.plot(kind="bar", stacked=True, figsize=(count + 1, 5))
    plt.legend(
        loc="lower left",
        frameon=False,
    )
    plt.legend(loc="upper left", bbox_to_anchor=(1, 1))
    plt.show()
In [283]:
### Function to plot distributions


def distribution_plot_wrt_target(data, predictor, target):

    fig, axs = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(12, 10))

    target_uniq = data[target].unique()

    axs[0, 0].set_title("Distribution of target for target=" + str(target_uniq[0]))
    sns.histplot(
        data=data[data[target] == target_uniq[0]],
        x=predictor,
        kde=True,
        ax=axs[0, 0],
        color="teal",
    )

    axs[0, 1].set_title("Distribution of target for target=" + str(target_uniq[1]))
    sns.histplot(
        data=data[data[target] == target_uniq[1]],
        x=predictor,
        kde=True,
        ax=axs[0, 1],
        color="orange",
    )

    axs[1, 0].set_title("Boxplot w.r.t target")
    sns.boxplot(data=data, x=target, y=predictor, ax=axs[1, 0], palette="gist_rainbow")

    axs[1, 1].set_title("Boxplot (without outliers) w.r.t target")
    sns.boxplot(
        data=data,
        x=target,
        y=predictor,
        ax=axs[1, 1],
        showfliers=False,
        palette="gist_rainbow",
    )

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

Bivariate Analysis¶

Observations of the Correlation plot¶

  • Ran Correlation Plot only on the numerical features in the dataframe
  • Resulting findings reveal that there is no correlation in the postive or negative direction between any of the features; in fact all of the correlation scores are from -0.01 to 0.01
In [295]:
#Create the correlation matrix and heatmap
#NOTE: Given that there are several categorical columns with multiple values, the correlation matrix will only capture the numerical values
#Create a list for only the numerical columns
numcols_list = ["CreditScore","Age","Tenure","Balance","EstimatedSalary"]
plt.figure(figsize=(15, 10))
cm = df[numcols_list].corr()
sns.heatmap(cm, annot=True, vmin=-1, vmax=1, fmt=".2f", cmap="Spectral")
plt.show()

Observations on Exited Vs Geography¶

  • The percentages of attrition/exited for each client location/geography are as follows:

  • Germany: ~35%

  • Spain: ~15%

  • France: ~15%

  • Non-exited/non-attrition is:

  • Germany: ~65%

  • Spain: ~85%

  • France: ~85%

Observations on Exited Vs Gender¶

  • The percentages of attrition/exited for each gender are:

  • Women: ~25%

  • Men: ~15%

  • Non-attrition/exited is:

  • Women: ~75%

  • Men: ~85%

Data Preprocessing¶

Dummy Variable Creation¶

In [ ]:
#Create a copy of the original Dataframe
df1 = df.copy()
#Reinitializing list of numerical value columens
numcols_list = ["CreditScore","Age","Tenure","Balance","EstimatedSalary"]
# Dropping RowNumber, CustomerId and Surname as they do not add value from an analytical standpoint as previously mentioned in observations and comments
df1 = df1.drop(['RowNumber', 'CustomerId', 'Surname'], axis=1)
#Create dummy variables for columns that are non-numeric or object designated
df1 = pd.get_dummies(df1,columns=df1.select_dtypes(include=["object"]).columns.tolist(),drop_first=True)
#Convert dummy variables to floating point numerical
df1 = df1.astype(float)
#Re-check of first 5 rows of the dataframe
df1.head()
Out[ ]:
CreditScore Age Tenure Balance NumOfProducts HasCrCard IsActiveMember EstimatedSalary Exited Geography_Germany Geography_Spain Gender_Male
0 619.0 42.0 2.0 0.00 1.0 1.0 1.0 101348.88 1.0 0.0 0.0 0.0

2 502.0 42.0 8.0 159660.80 3.0 1.0 0.0 113931.57 1.0 0.0 0.0 0.0

4 850.0 43.0 2.0 125510.82 1.0 1.0 1.0 79084.10 0.0 0.0 1.0 0.0

Train-validation-test Split¶

In [ ]:
#Defining Input and Output Dataframe
X = df1.drop(['Exited'],axis=1)
y = df1['Exited']

#Recheck the percentage breakdown of the Exited to non-Exited classes with the feature.
y.value_counts(normalize=True)
Out[ ]:
proportion
Exited
0.0 0.7963
1.0 0.2037

  • As previously mentioned the class split is ~80% non-attrition and ~20% attrition
In [ ]:
# Splitting the dataset into the Training and Testing set.
X_temp, X_test, y_temp, y_test = train_test_split(X, y, test_size = 0.2, random_state = 13) #,stratify=y,shuffle = True
# Splitting the dataset into the Training and Testing set.

X_train, X_val, y_train, y_val = train_test_split(X_temp, y_temp, test_size = 0.2, random_state = 13) #,stratify=y_temo, shuffle = True

#Print Shape of the Inputs
print(X_train.shape, X_val.shape, X_test.shape)

#Print Shapes of the Outputs
print(y_train.shape, y_val.shape, y_test.shape)
#Verify class split of the training, validation, and test set outputs
print(y_train.value_counts(normalize=True))
print(y_val.value_counts(normalize=True))
print(y_test.value_counts(normalize=True))

(6400, 11) (1600, 11) (2000, 11)
(6400,) (1600,) (2000,)

  • The Training, Validation, and Test set data maintains the same class breakdown for exited and non-exited

Data Normalization¶

In [ ]:
#Create Scaler
scale = StandardScaler()
#Transform and fit data to that scaler created
X_train[numcols_list] = scale.fit_transform(X_train[numcols_list])
X_val[numcols_list] = scale.transform(X_val[numcols_list])
X_test[numcols_list] = scale.transform(X_test[numcols_list])

Model Building¶

Model Evaluation Criterion¶

Write down the logic for choosing the metric that would be the best metric for this business scenario.

  • The best metric to use in this business scenario is Recall. Accuracy is also of strong importance to ensure proper fit, so it will be tracked.

  • The reason Recall is the best metric is because as a business, the bank is trying to predict the probability of churn in the client base and prevent that churn as much as possible as that churn is equivalent to revenue loss.

  • Now there are levels of willingness to pay for rentention efforts, as customer aqusition cost, customer support cost, and customer rentention cost in aggregate can put a bank into receiving a negative customer lifetime value at an indiviual level, and a negative profit margin,if not managed logically and within bounds of churn tolerance, due to the higher operational expinditure associated at the enterprise scale level due to the large client base.

Let's create a function for plotting the confusion matrix

In [ ]:
def make_confusion_matrix(actual_targets, predicted_targets):
    """
    To plot the confusion_matrix with percentages

    actual_targets: actual target (dependent) variable values
    predicted_targets: predicted target (dependent) variable values
    """
    cm = confusion_matrix(actual_targets, predicted_targets)
    labels = np.asarray(
        [
            ["{0:0.0f}".format(item) + "\n{0:.2%}".format(item / cm.flatten().sum())]
            for item in cm.flatten()
        ]
    ).reshape(cm.shape[0], cm.shape[1])

    plt.figure(figsize=(6, 4))
    sns.heatmap(cm, annot=labels, fmt="")
    plt.ylabel("True label")
    plt.xlabel("Predicted label")
In [ ]:
def plot(history, name):
    """
    Function to plot loss/accuracy

    history: an object which stores the metrics and losses.
    name: can be one of Loss or Accuracy
    """
    fig, ax = plt.subplots() #Creating a subplot with figure and axes.
    plt.plot(history.history[name]) #Plotting the train performance metric or train loss
    plt.plot(history.history['val_'+name]) #Plotting the validation performance metric or validation loss

    plt.title('Model ' + name.capitalize()) #Defining the title of the plot.
    plt.ylabel(name.capitalize()) #Capitalizing the first letter.
    plt.xlabel('Epoch') #Defining the label for the x-axis.
    fig.legend(['Train', 'Validation'], loc="outside right upper") #Defining the legend, loc controls the position of the legend.
In [ ]:
# defining a function to compute different metrics to check performance of a classification model built using statsmodels
def model_performance_classification(
    model, predictors, target, threshold=0.5
):
    """
    Function to compute different metrics to check classification model performance

    model: classifier
    predictors: independent variables
    target: dependent variable
    threshold: threshold for classifying the observation as class 1
    """

    # checking which probabilities are greater than threshold
    pred = model.predict(predictors) > threshold


    accuracy = accuracy_score(target, pred)  # to compute Accuracy
    recall = recall_score(target, pred, average='weighted')  # to compute Recall
    precision = precision_score(target, pred, average='weighted')  # to compute Precision
    f1 = f1_score(target, pred, average='weighted')  # to compute F1-score

    # creating a dataframe of metrics
    df_perf = pd.DataFrame(
        {"Accuracy": accuracy, "Recall": recall, "Precision": precision, "F1 Score": f1,},
        index=[0],
    )

    return df_perf

Initial Model Set-Up¶

Set up of performance and parameters dataframe for comparison of optimal model ultiimately chosen

In [ ]:
#Create a dataframe to store the results from all the models we build
#Defining the columns of the dataframe which are nothing but the hyper parameters and the metrics.
columns = ["# neurons - input layer","activation function - input layer","# hidden layers","# neurons - hidden layer","activation function - hidden layer ","# epochs","batch size","optimizer","learning rate", "momentum","weight initializer","regularization","train loss","validation loss","train accuracy","validation accuracy","train recall", "validation recall", "recall diff", "time (secs)"]

#Creating a pandas dataframe.
results = pd.DataFrame(columns=columns)
In [ ]:
#Creating a pandas dataframe.
subresults = pd.DataFrame(columns=columns)

Neural Network with SGD Optimizer Model_0A/ Manual Tuning¶

Initial Set Up of the Batch Size and Epochs Variables

In [ ]:
#Initialize batch size and epochs with each run; will update here if needed to optimize later on
batch_size = 32
epochs = 100
In [ ]:
# clears the current Keras session, resetting all layers and models previously created, freeing up memory and resources.
tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
#Sets the starting point/seed for every NN model built
np.random.seed()
random.seed()
tf.random.set_seed(13)
In [ ]:
#Initializing the neural network
model_0A = Sequential()
# Adding the input layer with 64 neurons and relu as activation function
model_0A.add(Dense(64, activation='relu', input_dim = X_train.shape[1]))
# Adding the hidden layer with 64 neurons and relu activation function
model_0A.add(Dense(64, activation='relu'))
# Addin output layer with 1 nueron and sigmoid activation function
model_0A.add(Dense(1, activation = 'sigmoid'))
In [ ]:
#Intiate the Optimizer with desired learning rate
optimizer = tf.keras.optimizers.SGD(learning_rate=0.001)

# Metric of Signinificance to track is Recall
metricA = 'accuracy'
metricR = 'recall'
In [ ]:
#Compile the model with binary cross entropy as loss function and accuracy and recall as the metrics.
model_0A.compile(loss='binary_crossentropy',optimizer=optimizer, metrics = [metricA, metricR])

Base Model Key Parameters chosen for manual tuning

  • Batch Size 32
  • Epochs 50
  • Input Layer: 64 Neurons, Relu Activation
  • Hidden Layer 1: 64 Neurons, Relu Activation
  • Output Layer: 1 Neuron, Sigmoid Activation
  • Optimizer: SGD, Learning Rate: 0.001
  • Loss Function: Binary Cross Entropy
In [ ]:
#Print a summary of the key parameters of the NN model created
model_0A.summary()

Model: "sequential"

In [ ]:
#Running the Fit of the NN on the Train and Validation  Data and tracking the time taken
start = time.time()

history_0A = model_0A.fit(
    X_train, y_train,
    batch_size= batch_size,
    validation_data=(X_val,y_val),
    epochs= epochs,
    verbose=1
)
end = time.time()
tt = end-start
print(f"Total training time: {tt} seconds")

SubResults Table

Base Model Key Parameters chosen for manual tuning

  • Batch Size 32
  • Epochs 100
  • Input Layer: 64 Neurons, Relu Activation
  • Hidden Layer 1: 64 Neurons, Relu Activation
  • Output Layer: 1 Neuron, Sigmoid Activation
  • Optimizer: SGD, Learning Rate: 0.001
  • Loss Function: Binary Cross Entropy

# subresults.drop(subresults.index[0], inplace=True)
#Enter in new parameter and performance information
subresults.loc[0] = [64, "relu", 1, 64, "relu", 100, 32, "sgd", 0.001, "-", "xavier", "-",
          history_0A.history["loss"][-1], history_0A.history["val_loss"][-1],
          history_0A.history["accuracy"][-1], history_0A.history["val_accuracy"][-1], history_0A.history['recall'][-1],
          history_0A.history['val_recall'][-1], history_0A.history['recall'][-1] - history_0A.history['val_recall'][-1],
          round(tt, 2)]
subresults
Out[ ]:
# neurons - input layer activation function - input layer # hidden layers # neurons - hidden layer activation function - hidden layer # epochs batch size optimizer learning rate momentum weight initializer regularization train loss validation loss train accuracy validation accuracy train recall validation recall recall diff time (secs)
0 64 relu 1 64 relu 100 32 sgd 0.001 - xavier - 0.410786 0.418288 0.819687 0.823125 0.230651 0.259939 -0.029288 78.25

Model Performance Improvement¶

Neural Network with SGD Optimizer Iterative Tuning¶

Neural Network with SGD Optimizer Model_0B/ Iterative Tuning¶

In [ ]:
# clears the current Keras session, resetting all layers and models previously created, freeing up memory and resources.
tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
#Fixing the seed for random number generators so that we can ensure we receive the same output everytime
np.random.seed(13)
random.seed(13)
tf.random.set_seed(13)
In [ ]:
def build_model(hp):
    model = keras.Sequential()

    # Input layer: Tune the number of neurons
    model.add(keras.layers.Input(shape=(11,)))
    model.add(keras.layers.Dense(
        units=hp.Int('input_neurons', min_value=16, max_value=256, step=16),
       activation=hp.Choice('input_activation', ['relu', 'tanh', 'sigmoid'])
    ))

    # Hidden layers: Tune the number of layers and neurons in each
    for i in range(hp.Int('num_hidden_layers', 1, 5)):  # Up to 5 hidden layers
        model.add(keras.layers.Dense(
            units=hp.Int(f'units_hidden_{i}', min_value=16, max_value=256, step=16),
            activation=hp.Choice('hidden_activation', ['relu', 'tanh', 'sigmoid'])
        ))

           # Get batch_size and epochs from hp object
    batch_size = hp.Choice('batch_size', [16, 32, 64, 128])
    epochs = hp.Int('epochs', 50, 200, step=10)


    # Output layer (binary classification)
    model.add(keras.layers.Dense(1, activation='sigmoid'))

    # Compile model with tunable learning rate
    model.compile(
        optimizer=keras.optimizers.SGD(
            learning_rate=hp.Float('learning_rate', 1e-4, 1e-1, sampling='log')
        ),
        loss='binary_crossentropy',
        metrics=['accuracy','recall']
    )
    return model
In [ ]:
# Initialize the tuner
tuner = kt.RandomSearch(
    build_model,
    objective='val_loss',  # Optimize for validation recall
    max_trials=10,            # Number of hyperparameter combinations to try
    executions_per_trial=1, # Number of model evaluations per trial
      directory='Model_0Bdir',
    project_name='Model_0B'

  )
In [ ]:
# #Run the search
# tuner.search(
#     X_train, y_train,
#     validation_data=(X_val, y_val)
# )

#Set up of tuner search using early stopping
early_stopping = keras.callbacks.EarlyStopping(monitor='val_loss', patience=5)
tuner.search(
    X_train, y_train,
    validation_data=(X_val, y_val),
    callbacks=[early_stopping]
)

Trial 10 Complete [00h 00m 03s]
val_loss: 0.6012369394302368

Best val_loss So Far: 0.432873010635376
Total elapsed time: 00h 00m 35s
In [ ]:
# Get the best hyperparameters
best_hps = tuner.get_best_hyperparameters(num_trials=1)[0]

print(f"""
The optimal number of input neurons: {best_hps.get('input_neurons')}
The optimal activation for input layer: {best_hps.get('input_activation')}
The optimal number of hidden layers: {best_hps.get('num_hidden_layers')}
The optimal number of neurons in hidden layers: {[best_hps.get(f'units_hidden_{i}') for i in range(best_hps.get('num_hidden_layers'))]}
The optimal activation for hidden layers: {best_hps.get('hidden_activation')}
The optimal learning rate: {best_hps.get('learning_rate')}
The optimal batch size: {best_hps.get('batch_size')}
The optimal number of epochs: {best_hps.get('epochs')}
""")

The optimal number of input neurons: 16
The optimal activation for input layer: tanh
The optimal number of hidden layers: 4
The optimal number of neurons in hidden layers: [240, 192, 208, 96]
The optimal activation for hidden layers: tanh
The optimal learning rate: 0.01132306820005268
The optimal batch size: 16
The optimal number of epochs: 130
In [ ]:
# Build the model with optimal model with parameters
model_0B = tuner.hypermodel.build(best_hps)

start = time.time()
# Train the model
history_0B = model_0B.fit(
    X_train, y_train,
    validation_data=(X_val, y_val),
    batch_size=best_hps.get('batch_size'),
    epochs=best_hps.get('epochs')
)

end = time.time()
tt = end-start
print(f"Total training time: {tt} seconds")

          history_0B.history["loss"][-1], history_0B.history["val_loss"][-1],

          round(tt, 2)]

Model_0 Chosen¶

In [ ]:
# Save the model and history chosen based on performance
model_0 = model_0B
history_0 = history_0B

Loss function

In [ ]:
plot(history_0, "loss")

Recall

In [ ]:
plot(history_0, "recall")

Accuracy

In [ ]:
plot(history_0, "accuracy")
In [ ]:
#Predicting the results using boundary of greater 0.5 to inlcude in the prediction set, i.e. confirmed exit
y_train_pred = model_0.predict(X_train)
y_train_pred = (y_train_pred > 0.5)
y_train_pred

0 240 tanh 4 [160, 64, 32, 240] tanh 100 32 sgd 0.028 - xavier - 0.316604 0.361055 0.871875 0.841875 0.509579 0.492355 0.017224 216.86

Neural Network with Adam Optimizer¶

In [ ]:
#Creating a pandas dataframe.
subresults = pd.DataFrame(columns=columns)

Model_1A/ Manual Tuning¶

In [ ]:
#Initialize batch size and epochs with each run; will update here if needed to optimize later on
batch_size = 16 # test 16,32,64 and 128
epochs = 50 # test 50, 100, 200
In [ ]:
# clears the current Keras session, resetting all layers and models previously created, freeing up memory and resources.
tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
#Fixing the seed for random number generators so that we can ensure we receive the same output everytime
np.random.seed(13)
random.seed(13)
tf.random.set_seed(13)
In [ ]:
#Initializing the neural network
model_1A = Sequential()
#input layer
model_1A.add(Dense(64,activation='relu',input_dim = X_train.shape[1]))
#Hidden Layer
model_1A.add(Dense(64,activation='relu'))
#Output Layer
model_1A.add(Dense(1, activation = 'relu'))
In [ ]:
#Initialize the Optimizer
optimizer = tf.keras.optimizers.Adam(learning_rate=0.001)

# Metric of Signinificance to track is Recall
metricA = 'accuracy'
metricR = 'recall'
In [ ]:
# Compile the model
model_1A.compile(loss='binary_crossentropy',optimizer=optimizer,metrics=[metricA, metricR])

Base Model Key Parameters:

  • Batch Size 16
  • Epochs 50
  • Input Layer: 64 Neurons, Relu Activation
  • Hidden Layer 1: 64 Neurons, Relu Activation
  • Output Layer: 1 Neuron, Sigmoid Activation
  • Optimizer: Adam, Learning Rate: 0.001
  • Loss Function: Binary Cross Entropy
In [ ]:
#Print a summary of the key parameters of the NN model created
model_1A.summary()

Model: "sequential"

┏━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓
┃ Layer (type)                         ┃ Output Shape                ┃         Param # ┃
┡━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┩
│ dense (Dense)                        │ (None, 64)                  │             768 │
├──────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┤
│ dense_1 (Dense)                      │ (None, 64)                  │           4,160 │
├──────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┤
│ dense_2 (Dense)                      │ (None, 1)                   │              65 │
└──────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────┴─────────────────┘

 Total params: 4,993 (19.50 KB)

 Trainable params: 4,993 (19.50 KB)

 Non-trainable params: 0 (0.00 B)
In [ ]:
#Running the Fit of the NN on the Train and Validation  Data and tracking the time taken

start = time.time()

history_1A = model_1A.fit(
    X_train,y_train,
    batch_size= batch_size,
    validation_data=(X_val,y_val),
    epochs= epochs,
    verbose=1
)

end = time.time()
tt = end-start
print(f"Total training time: {tt} seconds")

Epoch 1/50

Model_1B/ Iterative Tuning¶

In [ ]:
# clears the current Keras session, resetting all layers and models previously created, freeing up memory and resources.
tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
#Fixing the seed for random number generators so that we can ensure we receive the same output everytime
np.random.seed(13)
random.seed(13)
tf.random.set_seed(13)
In [ ]:
def build_model(hp):
    model = keras.Sequential()

    # Input layer: Tune the number of neurons
    model.add(keras.layers.Input(shape=(11,)))
    model.add(keras.layers.Dense(
        units=hp.Int('input_neurons', min_value=16, max_value=256, step=16),
        activation=hp.Choice('input_activation', ['relu', 'tanh', 'sigmoid'])
    ))

    # Hidden layers: Tune the number of layers and neurons in each
    for i in range(hp.Int('num_hidden_layers', 1, 5)):  # Up to 5 hidden layers
        model.add(keras.layers.Dense(
            units=hp.Int(f'units_hidden_{i}', min_value=16, max_value=256, step=16),
            activation=hp.Choice('hidden_activation', ['relu', 'tanh', 'sigmoid'])
        ))

           # Get batch_size and epochs from hp object
    batch_size = hp.Choice('batch_size', [16, 32, 64, 128])
    epochs = hp.Int('epochs', 50, 200, step=10)


    # Output layer (binary classification)
    model.add(keras.layers.Dense(1, activation='sigmoid'))

    # Compile model with tunable learning rate
    model.compile(
        optimizer=keras.optimizers.Adam(
            learning_rate=hp.Float('learning_rate', 1e-4, 1e-1, sampling='log')
        ),
        loss='binary_crossentropy',
        metrics=['accuracy','recall']
    )
    return model
In [ ]:
# Initialize the tuner
tuner = kt.RandomSearch(
    build_model,
    objective='val_loss',  # Optimize for validation recall
    max_trials=10,            # Number of hyperparameter combinations to try
    executions_per_trial=1, # Number of model evaluations per trial
      directory='Model_1Bdir',
    project_name='Model_1B'

  )
In [ ]:
# Run the search
tuner.search(
    X_train, y_train,
    validation_data=(X_val, y_val)
)

# #Set up of tuner search using early stopping
# early_stopping = keras.callbacks.EarlyStopping(monitor='val_loss', patience=3)
# tuner.search(
#     X_train, y_train,
#     validation_data=(X_val, y_val),
#     callbacks=[early_stopping]
# )

Trial 10 Complete [00h 00m 05s]
val_loss: 0.405635803937912

Best val_loss So Far: 0.4018106162548065
Total elapsed time: 00h 00m 38s
In [ ]:
# # Run the search
# tuner.search(
#     X_train, y_train,
#     validation_data=(X_val, y_val)
# )

#Set up of tuner search using early stopping
early_stopping = keras.callbacks.EarlyStopping(monitor='val_loss', patience=3)
tuner.search(
    X_train, y_train,
    validation_data=(X_val, y_val),
    callbacks=[early_stopping]
)
In [ ]:
# Get the best hyperparameters
best_hps = tuner.get_best_hyperparameters(num_trials=1)[0]

print(f"""
The optimal number of input neurons: {best_hps.get('input_neurons')}
The optimal activation for input layer: {best_hps.get('input_activation')}
The optimal number of hidden layers: {best_hps.get('num_hidden_layers')}
The optimal number of neurons in hidden layers: {[best_hps.get(f'units_hidden_{i}') for i in range(best_hps.get('num_hidden_layers'))]}
The optimal activation for hidden layers: {best_hps.get('hidden_activation')}
The optimal learning rate: {best_hps.get('learning_rate')}
The optimal batch size: {best_hps.get('batch_size')}
The optimal number of epochs: {best_hps.get('epochs')}
""")

The optimal number of input neurons: 256
The optimal activation for input layer: relu
The optimal number of hidden layers: 1
The optimal number of neurons in hidden layers: [208]
The optimal activation for hidden layers: tanh
The optimal learning rate: 0.007792822719960166
The optimal batch size: 32
The optimal number of epochs: 100
In [ ]:
# Build the model with optimal model with parameters
model_1B = tuner.hypermodel.build(best_hps)

start = time.time()
# Train the model
history_1B = model_1B.fit(
    X_train, y_train,
    validation_data=(X_val, y_val),
    batch_size=best_hps.get('batch_size'),
    epochs=best_hps.get('epochs')
)
end = time.time()
tt = end-start
print(f"Total training time: {tt} seconds")

Model_1 to Chosen¶

#Predicting the results using 0.5 as the threshold
y_train_pred = model_1.predict(X_train)
y_train_pred = (y_train_pred > 0.5)
y_train_pred

       [False],
       [False],
       [False]])

Classification report

In [ ]:
model_performance_classification(model_1, X_train, y_train)

#Calculating the confusion matrix
make_confusion_matrix(y_train, y_train_pred)
In [ ]:
#Calculating the confusion matrix
make_confusion_matrix(y_val, y_val_pred)

Results

In [ ]:
results.loc[1] = subresults.iloc[0]
results
Out[ ]:
# neurons - input layer activation function - input layer # hidden layers # neurons - hidden layer activation function - hidden layer # epochs batch size optimizer learning rate momentum weight initializer regularization train loss validation loss train accuracy validation accuracy train recall validation recall recall diff time (secs)
0 240 tanh 4 [160, 64, 32, 240] tanh 100 32 sgd 0.028 - xavier - 0.316604 0.361055 0.871875 0.841875 0.509579 0.492355 0.017224 216.86
1 64 relu 1 64 relu 50 16 adam 0.001 - xavier - 0.317832 0.541532 0.870625 0.845625 0.512644 0.590214 -0.07757 64.19

Neural Network with Adam Optimizer and Dropout¶

In [ ]:
#Creating a pandas dataframe.
subresults = pd.DataFrame(columns=columns)
Model_2A/ Manual Tuning¶
In [ ]:
#Initialize batch size and epochs with each run; will update here if needed to optimize later on
batch_size = 32
epochs = 100
In [ ]:
# clears the current Keras session, resetting all layers and models previously created, freeing up memory and resources.
tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
#Fixing the seed for random number generators so that we can ensure we receive the same output everytime
np.random.seed(13)
random.seed(13)
tf.random.set_seed(13)
In [ ]:
#Initializing the neural network
model_2A = Sequential()
#Adding the input layer with 64 neurons and relu as activation function
model_2A.add(Dense(64,activation='relu',input_dim = X_train.shape[1]))
# Add dropout with proportion of 0.2
model_2A.add(Dropout(0.2))
# Hidden Layer 1
model_2A.add(Dense(64,activation='relu'))
# Output Layer
model_2A.add(Dense(1, activation = 'sigmoid'))
In [ ]:
#Initiate the optimizer.
optimizer = tf.keras.optimizers.Adam(learning_rate=0.001)

# Metric of Signinificance to track is Recall
metricA = 'accuracy'
metricR = 'recall'
In [ ]:
## Compile the model with binary cross entropy as loss function and recall as the metric.
model_2A.compile(loss='binary_crossentropy',optimizer=optimizer,metrics=[metricA, metricR])

Base Model Key Parameters:

  • Batch Size 32
  • Epochs 100
  • Input Layer: 64 Neurons, Relu Activation
  • Hidden Layer 1: 64 Neurons, Relu Activation
  • Droppout: After input layer, 0.2
  • Output Layer: 1 Neuron, Sigmoid Activation
  • Optimizer: Adam, Learning Rate: 0.001
  • Loss Function: Binary Cross Entropy
In [ ]:
#Print a summary of the key parameters of the NN model created
model_2A.summary()

Model: "sequential"

┏━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓
┃ Layer (type)                         ┃ Output Shape                ┃         Param # ┃
┡━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┩
│ dense (Dense)                        │ (None, 64)                  │             768 │
├──────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┤
│ dropout (Dropout)                    │ (None, 64)                  │               0 │
├──────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┤
│ dense_1 (Dense)                      │ (None, 64)                  │           4,160 │
├──────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┤
│ dense_2 (Dense)                      │ (None, 1)                   │              65 │
└──────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────┴─────────────────┘

 Total params: 4,993 (19.50 KB)

 Trainable params: 4,993 (19.50 KB)

 Non-trainable params: 0 (0.00 B)
In [ ]:
#Running the Fit of the NN on the Train and Validation  Data and tracking the time taken

start = time.time()

history_2A = model_2A.fit(
    X_train,y_train,
    batch_size=batch_size,
    epochs=epochs,
    verbose=1,
    validation_data=(X_val,y_val)
)

end = time.time()
tt = end-start
print(f"Total training time: {tt} seconds")

Model_2B/ Iterative Tuning¶

In [ ]:
# clears the current Keras session, resetting all layers and models previously created, freeing up memory and resources.
tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
#Fixing the seed for random number generators so that we can ensure we receive the same output everytime
np.random.seed(13)
random.seed(13)
tf.random.set_seed(13)
In [ ]:
def build_model(hp):
    model = keras.Sequential()

    # Input layer with tunable neurons
    model.add(Dense(
        units=hp.Int('input_neurons', min_value=32, max_value=256, step=16),
        input_dim=11,
        activation=hp.Choice('input_activation', ['relu', 'tanh', 'sigmoid'])
    ))
    model.add(Dropout(hp.Float('dropout', min_value=0.0, max_value=0.5, step=0.1)))
    # Hidden layers with tunable parameters
    for i in range(hp.Int('num_hidden_layers', 1, 5)):  # Up to 5 hidden layers
        model.add(Dense(
            units=hp.Int(f'units_hidden_{i}', min_value=32, max_value=256, step=16),
            activation=hp.Choice('hidden_activation', ['relu', 'tanh', 'sigmoid'])
        ))
        # Apply dropout to hidden layers
        model.add(Dropout(hp.Float(f'dropout_{i}', min_value=0.0, max_value=0.5, step=0.1)))

        # Get batch_size and epochs from hp object
    batch_size = hp.Choice('batch_size', [16, 32, 64, 128])
    epochs = hp.Int('epochs', 50, 200, step=10)


    # Output layer (binary classification)
    model.add(keras.layers.Dense(1, activation='sigmoid'))

    # Compile model with tunable learning rate
    model.compile(
        optimizer=keras.optimizers.Adam(
            learning_rate=hp.Float('learning_rate', 1e-4, 1e-1, sampling='log')
        ),
        loss='binary_crossentropy',
        metrics=['accuracy','recall']
    )
    return model
In [ ]:
# Initialize the tuner
tuner = kt.RandomSearch(
    build_model,
    objective='val_loss',  # Optimize for validation recall
    max_trials=10,            # Number of hyperparameter combinations to try
    executions_per_trial=1, # Number of model evaluations per trial
      directory='Model_2Bdir',
    project_name='Model_2B'

  )
In [ ]:
# # Run the search
# tuner.search(
#     X_train, y_train,
#     validation_data=(X_val, y_val)
# )

#Set up of tuner search using early stopping
early_stopping = keras.callbacks.EarlyStopping(monitor='val_loss', patience=3)
tuner.search(
    X_train, y_train,
    validation_data=(X_val, y_val),
    callbacks=[early_stopping]
)

Trial 10 Complete [00h 00m 06s]
val_loss: 0.49609431624412537

Best val_loss So Far: 0.4083448648452759
Total elapsed time: 00h 01m 04s
In [ ]:
# Get the best hyperparameters
best_hps = tuner.get_best_hyperparameters(num_trials=1)[0]


print(f"""
Optimal Input Neurons: {best_hps.get('input_neurons')}
Optimal Input Activation: {best_hps.get('input_activation')}
Optimal Number of Hidden Layers: {best_hps.get('num_hidden_layers')}
Optimal Neurons per Hidden Layer: {[best_hps.get(f'units_hidden_{i}') for i in range(best_hps.get('num_hidden_layers'))]}
Optimal Activation for Hidden Layers: {best_hps.get('hidden_activation')}
Optimal Dropout Rates: {[best_hps.get(f'dropout_{i}') if f'dropout_{i}' in best_hps else None for i in range(best_hps.get('num_hidden_layers'))] if best_hps.get('num_hidden_layers') > 0 else []}
Optimal Learning Rate: {best_hps.get('learning_rate')}
Optimal Batch Size: {best_hps.get('batch_size')}
Optimal Number of Epochs: {best_hps.get('epochs')}
""")

Optimal Input Neurons: 256
Optimal Input Activation: relu
Optimal Number of Hidden Layers: 1
Optimal Neurons per Hidden Layer: [256]
Optimal Activation for Hidden Layers: sigmoid
Optimal Dropout Rates: [0.2]
Optimal Learning Rate: 0.007819818963829321
Optimal Batch Size: 128
Optimal Number of Epochs: 50
In [ ]:
# Build the model with optimal hyperparameters
model_2B = tuner.hypermodel.build(best_hps)

start = time.time()

# Train the model
history_2B = model_2B.fit(
    X_train, y_train,
    validation_data=(X_val, y_val),
    batch_size=best_hps.get('batch_size'),
    epochs=best_hps.get('epochs')
)

end = time.time()
tt = end-start
print(f"Total training time: {tt} seconds")

Model_2 Chosen¶

In [ ]:
# Save the model and history
model_2 = model_2B
history_2 = history_2B

Loss function

In [ ]:
plot(history_2, "loss")

Recall

In [ ]:
plot(history_2, "recall")

Accuracy

In [ ]:
plot(history_2, "accuracy")
In [ ]:
#Predicting the results using 0.5 as the threshold.
y_train_pred = model_2.predict(X_train)
y_train_pred = (y_train_pred > 0.5)
y_train_pred

Neural Network with Balanced Data (by applying SMOTE) and SGD Optimizer¶

In [ ]:
#Creating a pandas dataframe.
subresults = pd.DataFrame(columns=columns)

SMOTE/oversampling will be applied to balance the dataset

In [ ]:
os  = SMOTE(random_state=13)
X_train_over, y_train_over= os.fit_resample(X_train,y_train)
print('After Over Sampling, the shape of train_X: {}'.format(X_train_over.shape))
print('After Over Sampling, the shape of train_y: {} \n'.format(y_train_over.shape))

After Over Sampling, the shape of train_X: (10190, 11)
After Over Sampling, the shape of train_y: (10190,)

Model_3A/ Manual Tuning¶

In [ ]:
#Initialize batch size and epochs with each run; will update here if needed to optimize later on
batch_size = 32
epochs = 100

Let's build a model with the balanced dataset

In [ ]:
# clears the current Keras session, resetting all layers and models previously created, freeing up memory and resources.
tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
#Fixing the seed for random number generators so that we can ensure we receive the same output everytime
np.random.seed(13)
random.seed(13)
tf.random.set_seed(13)
In [ ]:
#Initializing the model
model_3A = Sequential()
#Inout Layer
model_3A.add(Dense(64,activation='relu',input_dim = X_train_over.shape[1]))
#Hidden Layer 1
model_3A.add(Dense(64,activation='relu'))
#Output Layer
model_3A.add(Dense(1, activation = 'sigmoid'))
In [ ]:
#Initate the optimizer
optimizer = tf.keras.optimizers.SGD(learning_rate=0.001)

# Metric of Signinificance to track is Recall
metricA = 'accuracy'
metricR = 'recall'
In [ ]:
# Compile the model with binary cross entropy as loss function and recall as the metric
model_3A.compile(loss='binary_crossentropy',optimizer=optimizer,metrics=[metricA, metricR])

Base Model Key Parameters:

  • Batch Size 32
  • Epochs 100
  • Input Layer: 64 Neurons, Relu Activation
  • Hidden Layer 1: 64 Neurons, Relu Activation
  • Output Layer: 1 Neuron, Sigmoid Activation
  • Optimizer: SGD, Learning Rate: 0.001
  • Loss Function: Binary Cross Entropy
In [ ]:
#Print a summary of the key parameters of the NN model created
model_3A.summary()

Model: "sequential"

In [ ]:
#Running the Fit of the NN on the Train and Validation  Data and tracking the time taken

start = time.time()

history_3A = model_3A.fit(
    X_train_over, y_train_over,
    batch_size= batch_size,
    epochs= epochs,
    verbose=1,
    validation_data = (X_val,y_val)
)

end = time.time()
tt = end-start
print(f"Total training time: {tt} seconds")

Model_3B/ Iterative Tuning¶

In [ ]:
# clears the current Keras session, resetting all layers and models previously created, freeing up memory and resources.
tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
#Fixing the seed for random number generators so that we can ensure we receive the same output everytime
np.random.seed(13)
random.seed(13)
tf.random.set_seed(13)
In [ ]:
def build_model(hp):
    model = keras.Sequential()

    # Input layer: Tune the number of neurons
    model.add(keras.layers.Input(shape=(11,)))
    model.add(keras.layers.Dense(
        units=hp.Int('input_neurons', min_value=16, max_value=256, step=16),
        activation=hp.Choice('input_activation', ['relu', 'tanh', 'sigmoid'])
    ))

    # Hidden layers: Tune the number of layers and neurons in each
    for i in range(hp.Int('num_hidden_layers', 1, 5)):  # Up to 5 hidden layers
        model.add(keras.layers.Dense(
            units=hp.Int(f'units_hidden_{i}', min_value=16, max_value=256, step=16),
            activation=hp.Choice('hidden_activation', ['relu', 'tanh', 'sigmoid'])
        ))

           # Get batch_size and epochs from hp object
    batch_size = hp.Choice('batch_size', [16, 32, 64, 128])
    epochs = hp.Int('epochs', 50, 200, step=10)


    # Output layer (binary classification)
    model.add(keras.layers.Dense(1, activation='sigmoid'))

    # Compile model with tunable learning rate
    model.compile(
        optimizer=keras.optimizers.SGD(
            learning_rate=hp.Float('learning_rate', 1e-4, 1e-1, sampling='log')
        ),
        loss='binary_crossentropy',
        metrics=['accuracy','recall']
    )
    return model
In [ ]:
# Initialize the tuner
tuner = kt.RandomSearch(
    build_model,
    objective='val_loss',  # Optimize for validation recall
    max_trials=10,            # Number of hyperparameter combinations to try
    executions_per_trial=1, # Number of model evaluations per trial
      directory='Model_3Bdir',
    project_name='Model_3B'

  )
In [ ]:
# # Run the search
# tuner.search(
#     X_train_over, y_train_over,
#     validation_data=(X_val, y_val)
# )

#Set up of tuner search using early stopping
early_stopping = keras.callbacks.EarlyStopping(monitor='val_loss', patience=3)
tuner.search(
    X_train_over, y_train_over,
    validation_data=(X_val, y_val),
    callbacks=[early_stopping]
)

Trial 10 Complete [00h 00m 06s]
val_loss: 0.6701404452323914

Best val_loss So Far: 0.5633158683776855
Total elapsed time: 00h 00m 49s
In [ ]:
# Get the best hyperparameters
best_hps = tuner.get_best_hyperparameters(num_trials=1)[0]

print(f"""
The optimal number of input neurons: {best_hps.get('input_neurons')}
The optimal activation for input layer: {best_hps.get('input_activation')}
The optimal number of hidden layers: {best_hps.get('num_hidden_layers')}
The optimal number of neurons in hidden layers: {[best_hps.get(f'units_hidden_{i}') for i in range(best_hps.get('num_hidden_layers'))]}
The optimal activation for hidden layers: {best_hps.get('hidden_activation')}
The optimal learning rate: {best_hps.get('learning_rate')}
The optimal batch size: {best_hps.get('batch_size')}
The optimal number of epochs: {best_hps.get('epochs')}
""")

The optimal number of input neurons: 240
The optimal activation for input layer: tanh
The optimal number of hidden layers: 4
The optimal number of neurons in hidden layers: [160, 64, 32, 240]
The optimal activation for hidden layers: tanh
The optimal learning rate: 0.028405838507664222
The optimal batch size: 32
The optimal number of epochs: 100
In [ ]:
# Build the model with optimal model with parameters
model_3B = tuner.hypermodel.build(best_hps)

# Train the model
history_3B = model_3B.fit(
    X_train_over, y_train_over,
    validation_data=(X_val, y_val),
    batch_size=best_hps.get('batch_size'),
    epochs=best_hps.get('epochs')
)

Epoch 100/100

Model_3 Chosen¶

In [ ]:
# Save the model and history
model_3 = model_3A
history_3 = history_3A

In [ ]:
plot(history_3, "loss")

Recall

In [ ]:
plot(history_3, "recall")

Accuracy

In [ ]:
plot(history_3, "accuracy")
In [ ]:
#Predicting the results using 0.5 as the threshold.
y_train_pred = model_3.predict(X_train_over)
y_train_pred = (y_train_pred > 0.5)
y_train_pred

Neural Network with Balanced Data (by applying SMOTE) and Adam Optimizer¶

In [ ]:
#Creating a pandas dataframe.
subresults = pd.DataFrame(columns=columns)

Model_4A/ Manual Tuning¶

In [ ]:
#Initialize batch size and epochs with each run; will update here if needed to optimize later on
batch_size = 32
epochs = 100

Let's build a model with the balanced dataset

In [ ]:
# clears the current Keras session, resetting all layers and models previously created, freeing up memory and resources.
tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
#Fixing the seed for random number generators so that we can ensure we receive the same output everytime
np.random.seed(13)
random.seed(13)
tf.random.set_seed(13)
In [ ]:
#Initializing the model
model_4A = Sequential()
#Inout Layer
model_4A.add(Dense(64,activation='relu',input_dim = X_train_over.shape[1]))
#Hidden Layer 1
model_4A.add(Dense(64,activation='relu'))
#Output Layer
model_4A.add(Dense(1, activation = 'sigmoid'))
In [ ]:
#Initialize Adam as the optimizer.
optimizer = tf.keras.optimizers.Adam(learning_rate=0.001)

# Metric of Signinificance to track is Recall
metricA = 'accuracy'
metricR = 'recall'
In [ ]:
#Compile the model with binary cross entropy as loss function and accuracy and recall as the metric
model_4A.compile(loss='binary_crossentropy',optimizer=optimizer,metrics=[metricA, metricR])

Base Model Key Parameters:

  • Batch Size 32
  • Epochs 100
  • Input Layer: 64 Neurons, Relu Activation
  • Hidden Layer 1: 64 Neurons, Relu Activation
  • Output Layer: 1 Neuron, Sigmoid Activation
  • Optimizer: Adam, Learning Rate: 0.001
  • Loss Function: Binary Cross Entropy
In [ ]:
#Print a summary of the key parameters of the NN model created
model_4A.summary()

Model: "sequential"

┏━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓
┃ Layer (type)                         ┃ Output Shape                ┃         Param # ┃
┡━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┩
│ dense (Dense)                        │ (None, 64)                  │             768 │
├──────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┤
│ dense_1 (Dense)                      │ (None, 64)                  │           4,160 │
├──────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┤
│ dense_2 (Dense)                      │ (None, 1)                   │              65 │
└──────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────┴─────────────────┘

 Total params: 4,993 (19.50 KB)

 Trainable params: 4,993 (19.50 KB)

 Non-trainable params: 0 (0.00 B)
In [ ]:
#Running the Fit of the NN on the Train and Validation  Data and tracking the time taken

start = time.time()

history_4A = model_4A.fit(
    X_train_over,y_train_over,
    batch_size= batch_size,
    epochs= epochs,
    verbose=1,
    validation_data = (X_val,y_val)
)

end = time.time()
tt = end-start
print(f"Total training time: {tt} seconds")

Model_4B/ Iterative Tuning¶

In [ ]:
# clears the current Keras session, resetting all layers and models previously created, freeing up memory and resources.
tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
#Fixing the seed for random number generators so that we can ensure we receive the same output everytime
np.random.seed(13)
random.seed(13)
tf.random.set_seed(13)
In [ ]:
def build_model(hp):
    model = keras.Sequential()

    # Input layer: Tune the number of neurons
    model.add(keras.layers.Input(shape=(11,)))
    model.add(keras.layers.Dense(
        units=hp.Int('input_neurons', min_value=16, max_value=256, step=16),
        activation=hp.Choice('input_activation', ['relu', 'tanh', 'sigmoid'])
    ))

    # Hidden layers: Tune the number of layers and neurons in each
    for i in range(hp.Int('num_hidden_layers', 1, 5)):  # Up to 5 hidden layers
        model.add(keras.layers.Dense(
            units=hp.Int(f'units_hidden_{i}', min_value=16, max_value=256, step=16),
            activation=hp.Choice('hidden_activation', ['relu', 'tanh', 'sigmoid'])
        ))

           # Get batch_size and epochs from hp object
    batch_size = hp.Choice('batch_size', [16, 32, 64, 128])
    epochs = hp.Int('epochs', 50, 200, step=10)


    # Output layer (binary classification)
    model.add(keras.layers.Dense(1, activation='sigmoid'))

    # Compile model with tunable learning rate
    model.compile(
        optimizer=keras.optimizers.Adam(
            learning_rate=hp.Float('learning_rate', 1e-4, 1e-1, sampling='log')
        ),
        loss='binary_crossentropy',
        metrics=['accuracy','recall']
    )
    return model
In [ ]:
# Initialize the tuner
tuner = kt.RandomSearch(
    build_model,
    objective='val_loss',  # Optimize for validation recall
    max_trials=10,            # Number of hyperparameter combinations to try
    executions_per_trial=1, # Number of model evaluations per trial
      directory='Model_4Bdir',
    project_name='Model_4B'

  )
In [ ]:
# # Run the search
# tuner.search(
#     X_train_over, y_train_over,
#     validation_data=(X_val, y_val)
# )

#Set up of tuner search using early stopping
early_stopping = keras.callbacks.EarlyStopping(monitor='val_loss', patience=3)
tuner.search(
    X_train_over, y_train_over,
    validation_data=(X_val, y_val),
    callbacks=[early_stopping]
)

In [ ]:
# Get the best hyperparameters
best_hps = tuner.get_best_hyperparameters(num_trials=1)[0]

print(f"""
The optimal number of input neurons: {best_hps.get('input_neurons')}
The optimal activation for input layer: {best_hps.get('input_activation')}
The optimal number of hidden layers: {best_hps.get('num_hidden_layers')}
The optimal number of neurons in hidden layers: {[best_hps.get(f'units_hidden_{i}') for i in range(best_hps.get('num_hidden_layers'))]}
The optimal activation for hidden layers: {best_hps.get('hidden_activation')}
The optimal learning rate: {best_hps.get('learning_rate')}
The optimal batch size: {best_hps.get('batch_size')}
The optimal number of epochs: {best_hps.get('epochs')}
""")

The optimal number of input neurons: 16
The optimal activation for input layer: tanh
The optimal number of hidden layers: 4
The optimal number of neurons in hidden layers: [240, 192, 208, 96]
The optimal activation for hidden layers: tanh
The optimal learning rate: 0.01132306820005268
The optimal batch size: 16
The optimal number of epochs: 130
In [ ]:
# Build the model with optimal model with parameters
model_4B = tuner.hypermodel.build(best_hps)

start = time.time()

# Train the model
history_4B = model_4B.fit(
    X_train_over, y_train_over,
    validation_data=(X_val, y_val),
    batch_size=best_hps.get('batch_size'),
    epochs=best_hps.get('epochs')
)

end = time.time()
tt = end-start
print(f"Total training time: {tt} seconds")

Model_4 Chosen¶

In [ ]:
# Save the model and history
model_4 = model_4A
history_4 = history_4A

Loss function

In [ ]:
plot(history_4, "loss")

Recall

In [ ]:
plot(history_4, "recall")

Accuracy

In [ ]:
plot(history_4, "accuracy")
In [ ]:
#Predicting the results using 0.5 as the threshold
y_train_pred = model_4.predict(X_train_over)
y_train_pred = (y_train_pred > 0.5)
y_train_pred

model_performance_classification(model_4, X_train_over, y_train_over)

cr=classification_report(y_val,y_val_pred)
print(cr)

results.loc[4]= subresults.iloc[0]
results

0 240 tanh 4 [160, 64, 32, 240] tanh 100 32 sgd 0.028 - xavier - 0.316604 0.361055 0.871875 0.841875 0.509579 0.492355 0.017224 216.86
1 64 relu 1 64 relu 50 16 adam 0.001 - xavier - 0.317832 0.541532 0.870625 0.845625 0.512644 0.590214 -0.07757 64.19
2 64 relu 1 64 relu 100 32 adam 0.001 - xavier Dropout [0.2] 0.282304 0.373828 0.88375 0.846875 0.56705 0.547401 0.019649 113.75
3 64 relu 1 64 relu 100 32 sgd 0.001 - xavier - 0.516398 0.521228 0.746614 0.72375 0.779392 0.706422 0.07297 133.56
4 64 relu 1 64 relu 100 32 adam 0.001 - xavier - 0.177614 0.706794 0.931698 0.764375 0.940137 0.629969 0.310168 168.59

Neural Network with Balanced Data (by applying SMOTE), Adam Optimizer, and Dropout¶

In [ ]:
#Creating a pandas dataframe.
subresults = pd.DataFrame(columns=columns)

Model_5A/ Manual Tuning¶

In [ ]:
#Initialize batch size and epochs with each run; will update here if needed to optimize later on
batch_size = 32
epochs = 100
In [ ]:
# clears the current Keras session, resetting all layers and models previously created, freeing up memory and resources.
tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
#Fixing the seed for random number generators so that we can ensure we receive the same output everytime
np.random.seed(13)
random.seed(13)
tf.random.set_seed(13)
In [ ]:
#Initializing the model
model_5A = Sequential()
#Input Layer
model_5A.add(Dense(64,activation='tanh',input_dim = X_train_over.shape[1]))
#Add Dropout 1
model_5A.add(Dropout(0.2))
#Hidden Layer 1
model_5A.add(Dense(64,activation='relu'))
#Output Layer
model_5A.add(Dense(1, activation = 'sigmoid'))
In [ ]:
#Initialize the optimizer
optimizer = tf.keras.optimizers.Adam(learning_rate=0.001)

# Metric of Signinificance to track is Recall
metricA = 'accuracy'
metricR = 'recall'
In [ ]:
# Compile the model with binary cross entropy as loss function and recall as the metric
model_5A.compile(loss='binary_crossentropy',optimizer=optimizer,metrics=[metricA, metricR])

Base Model Key Parameters:

  • Batch Size 32
  • Epochs 100
  • Input Layer: 64 Neurons, Relu Activation
  • Hidden Layer 1: 64 Neurons, Relu Activation
  • Output Layer: 1 Neuron, Sigmoid Activation
  • Dropout: After Input, 0.2
  • Optimizer: Adam, Learning Rate: 0.001
  • Loss Function: Binary Cross Entropy
In [ ]:
#Print a summary of the key parameters of the NN model created
model_5A.summary()

Model: "sequential"

┏━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┓
┃ Layer (type)                         ┃ Output Shape                ┃         Param # ┃
┡━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┩
│ dense (Dense)                        │ (None, 64)                  │             768 │
├──────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┤
│ dropout (Dropout)                    │ (None, 64)                  │               0 │
├──────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┤
│ dense_1 (Dense)                      │ (None, 64)                  │           4,160 │
├──────────────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────────┼─────────────────┤
│ dense_2 (Dense)                      │ (None, 1)                   │              65 │
└──────────────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────────────┴─────────────────┘

 Total params: 4,993 (19.50 KB)

 Trainable params: 4,993 (19.50 KB)

 Non-trainable params: 0 (0.00 B)
In [ ]:
#Running the Fit of the NN on the Train and Validation  Data and tracking the time taken
start = time.time()

history_5A = model_5A.fit(
    X_train_over,y_train_over,
    batch_size= batch_size,
    epochs= epochs,
    verbose=1,
    validation_data = (X_val,y_val))

end = time.time()
tt = end-start
print(f"Total training time: {tt} seconds")

Model_5B/ Iterative Tuning¶

In [ ]:
# clears the current Keras session, resetting all layers and models previously created, freeing up memory and resources.
tf.keras.backend.clear_session()
#Fixing the seed for random number generators so that we can ensure we receive the same output everytime
np.random.seed(13)
random.seed(13)
tf.random.set_seed(13)
In [ ]:
def build_model(hp):
    model = keras.Sequential()

    # Input layer with tunable neurons
    model.add(Dense(
        units=hp.Int('input_neurons', min_value=32, max_value=256, step=16),
        input_dim=11,
        activation=hp.Choice('input_activation', ['relu', 'tanh', 'sigmoid'])
    ))
    model.add(Dropout(hp.Float('dropout', min_value=0.0, max_value=0.5, step=0.1)))
    # Hidden layers with tunable parameters
    for i in range(hp.Int('num_hidden_layers', 1, 5)):  # Up to 5 hidden layers
        model.add(Dense(
            units=hp.Int(f'units_hidden_{i}', min_value=32, max_value=256, step=16),
            activation=hp.Choice('hidden_activation', ['relu', 'tanh', 'sigmoid'])
        ))
        # Apply dropout to hidden layers
        model.add(Dropout(hp.Float(f'dropout_{i}', min_value=0.0, max_value=0.5, step=0.1)))

        # Get batch_size and epochs from hp object
    batch_size = hp.Choice('batch_size', [16, 32, 64, 128])
    epochs = hp.Int('epochs', 50, 200, step=10)


    # Output layer (binary classification)
    model.add(keras.layers.Dense(1, activation='sigmoid'))

    # Compile model with tunable learning rate
    model.compile(
        optimizer=keras.optimizers.Adam(
            learning_rate=hp.Float('learning_rate', 1e-4, 1e-1, sampling='log')
        ),
        loss='binary_crossentropy',
        metrics=['accuracy','recall']
    )
    return model
In [ ]:
# Initialize the tuner
tuner = kt.RandomSearch(
    build_model,
    objective='val_loss',  # Optimize for validation recall
    max_trials=10,            # Number of hyperparameter combinations to try
    executions_per_trial=1, # Number of model evaluations per trial
      directory='Model_5Bdir',
    project_name='Model_5B'

  )
In [ ]:
# # Run the search
# tuner.search(
#     X_train_over, y_train_over,
#     validation_data=(X_val, y_val)
# )

#Set up of tuner search using early stopping
early_stopping = keras.callbacks.EarlyStopping(monitor='val_loss', patience=3)
tuner.search(
    X_train_over, y_train_over,
    validation_data=(X_val, y_val),
    callbacks=[early_stopping]
)

Trial 10 Complete [00h 00m 11s]
val_loss: 0.5507768988609314

Best val_loss So Far: 0.5232166051864624
Total elapsed time: 00h 01m 28s
In [ ]:
# Get the best hyperparameters
best_hps = tuner.get_best_hyperparameters(num_trials=1)[0]


print(f"""
Optimal Input Neurons: {best_hps.get('input_neurons')}
Optimal Input Activation: {best_hps.get('input_activation')}
Optimal Number of Hidden Layers: {best_hps.get('num_hidden_layers')}
Optimal Neurons per Hidden Layer: {[best_hps.get(f'units_hidden_{i}') for i in range(best_hps.get('num_hidden_layers'))]}
Optimal Activation for Hidden Layers: {best_hps.get('hidden_activation')}
Optimal Dropout Rates: {[best_hps.get(f'dropout_{i}') if f'dropout_{i}' in best_hps else None for i in range(best_hps.get('num_hidden_layers'))] if best_hps.get('num_hidden_layers') > 0 else []}
Optimal Learning Rate: {best_hps.get('learning_rate')}
Optimal Batch Size: {best_hps.get('batch_size')}
Optimal Number of Epochs: {best_hps.get('epochs')}
""")

Optimal Input Neurons: 160
Optimal Input Activation: tanh
Optimal Number of Hidden Layers: 2
Optimal Neurons per Hidden Layer: [224, 112]
Optimal Activation for Hidden Layers: relu
Optimal Dropout Rates: [0.0, 0.4]
Optimal Learning Rate: 0.00025795109909605935
Optimal Batch Size: 128
Optimal Number of Epochs: 130
In [ ]:
# Build the model with optimal hyperparameters
model_5B = tuner.hypermodel.build(best_hps)

start = time.time()

# Train the model
history_5B = model_5B.fit(
    X_train_over, y_train_over,
    validation_data=(X_val, y_val),
    batch_size=best_hps.get('batch_size'),
    epochs=best_hps.get('epochs')
)

end = time.time()
tt = end-start
print(f"Total training time: {tt} seconds")

Model_5 Chosen¶

In [ ]:
# Save the model and history
model_5 = model_5A
history_5 = history_5A

Loss function

In [ ]:
plot(history_5, "loss")

Recall

In [ ]:
plot(history_5, "recall")

Accuracy

In [ ]:
plot(history_5, "accuracy")
In [ ]:
#Predicting the results using 0.5 as the threshold
y_train_pred = model_5.predict(X_train_over)
y_train_pred = (y_train_pred > 0.5)
y_train_pred

#Predicting the results using 0.5 as the threshold
y_val_pred = model_5.predict(X_val)
y_val_pred = (y_val_pred > 0.5)
y_val_pred

Model Performance Comparison and Final Model Selection¶

In [ ]:
#Print a full list of the performance results obtained
print('Parameters and Performance of each NN created, trained, and tuned')
results

In [ ]:
#Best Model and History chosen based on performance of key metric, Recall, and inclusive of lesser consideration due to Accuracy
chosen_model = model_3
chosen_history = history_3
In [ ]:
#Predicting the results using 0.5 as the threshold
y_test_pred = chosen_model.predict(X_test)
y_test_pred = (y_test_pred > 0.5)
print(y_test_pred)

Spot Check on a Single Data Point from the Data¶

# choosing an arbitrary data point and initializing the pruned model chosen in a new variable
applicant_details = X_test.iloc[:1, :]
# Select a random data point
print(applicant_details)

# making a prediction on the chosen NN model
approval_prediction = chosen_model.predict(applicant_details)
print(approval_prediction)

[[2.9508223e-09]]

Actionable Insights and Business Recommendations¶

  • Data Preprocessing and EDA:
  • It was determined that the Surname column provided no benefit in addition to RowID and CustomerIDs number
  • The data was normalized to allowed for easier input into the built NN Models and dummy variables were created for the binary categorical variables
  • No strong correlation in either direction for any of the numerical value features
  • Those that "Exited" the bank was roughly ~20% of the current customer base, with ~80% retained/staying as clients
  • Germany was the highest region with attrition at ~25-30% of client base
  • Women were more likely to exit than men, with ~25% churn compared to ~15%
  • Those deemed not active members are more likely to churn than active members at ~25-30% churn to ~15-20%
  • The distribution of age if those that do churn is more normal distributed from 20 to ~75 years of age; while those retained are highlt right skewed, i.e. normal distribution from 20 to 50 years of age, and then a long tail out to 90 years of age, shows higher age may lead to higher churn
  • Number of products for that churn was highest at 1 and dramtically drops as the products increase, shows that bundling may be a strong tactic to keep clients at the bank and may further increase activity too
  • All other features had no strong inclination based on distributions displayed on the likelihood of exit.

- Model Success Criterion, Building, and Results

  • Success criterion was decided to be based on Recall, specifically validation recall due to the greater detriment for the bank in letting clients churn as opposed to be more judicious in who is targeted with rentention efforts by the bank

  • A dual approach of manual tuning and iterative tuning of hyperparamters using Keras tuning was chosen.

  • The objective parameter chosen was actualyl validation loss due to the close correlation of optimal value for loss tied to recall. Searching for recall only led to performance tables where validation accuracy was minimized and observable validation loss dramatically spiked over time compared to its starting point and to the training losss observed.

  • NOTE: Limitations quickly presented themselves when doing iterative tuning for the NN models as long runtimes and depletion of usable processing and memory at times was seen. Hence there was some sacrifice in optimization of the models, with fewer trials ran due to CPU/GPU limitations

  • A few surprising items of note, but maybe not due to the aforementioned limitations:

    1. The iterative tuned models did not always perform the best
    1. The plots to forecast the loss, recall, and accuracy were not always smooth even after tuning; still large amount of noise even though learning rate was decreased to converge slower in same cases

  • The optimal model ultimately chosen was the manual tuned Model 3, which was the NN with the SGD optimizer and trained on the oversampled data set.

  • The optimal hyper parameters found for that model were:

  • Batch Size 32

  • Epochs 100

  • Input Layer: 64 Neurons, Relu Activation

  • Hidden Layer 1: 64 Neurons, Relu Activation

  • Output Layer: 1 Neuron, Sigmoid Activation

  • Optimizer: SGD, Learning Rate: 0.001

  • Loss Function: Binary Cross Entropy

    • The optimal peformance for it on the validation loss, recall and accuracy were:

  • Loss: 0.52

  • Recall: 0.71

  • Accuracy: 0.75

  • Time: 134 seconds

This shows good generalization

  • The performance on the test data when running the model on it was:
  • Recall: 0.79
  • Accuracy: 0.79

This shows good generalization

NOTE: Greater processing and memory cabilities can lead to stronger models developed

- Business Reccommendations in conclusion on prior insights are below:

  • Focus on bundling products to provide to customers, as it decreases churn
  • Focus greatest effort on retaining clients in Germany, due to largest amount of churn seen there.
  • Focus on retaining elderly clients past 50, as churn is higher for that group
  • Focusing on increasing opportunities to engage with the bank by the client, higher activity leads to higher retention
  • Use the NN model chosen, set churn threshold at greater than 50% to maxmize retention strategy

Power Ahead
